gate_classes.meaning: The family may enter bounded first-wave Ζ-A planning because it can remain narrow, reversible, and proof-bearing.
The family may enter bounded first-wave Ζ-A planning only under stronger guardrails because material blockers still constrain scope.
The family is not ordinary first-wave scope and may only be considered under explicit review.
The family is deferred out of first-wave scope and may only be reconsidered after earlier proof or convergence gates narrow materially.
The family is not admitted because the open blocker stack is too central to bounded entry.
The family remains outside bounded near-term Ζ-A entry entirely.

gate_criteria.meaning: Unresolved blocker entries from Ζ-P0 remain binding.
The family must have explicit governing law before any admission is possible.
The prerequisite stack from Ζ-P1 must be satisfiable for the bounded scope being admitted.
Unresolved replication, replay, proof-anchor, or cutover proof narrows or blocks admission.
Unresolved distributed trust, revocation, or publication execution posture narrows or blocks trust-bearing families.
Unresolved live-cutover receipt pipeline realization constrains evidence-bearing families.
Unresolved hotswap and distributed-authority cutover proof constrains runtime-adjacent families.
First-wave, later-wave, and non-admissible posture from Ζ-P1 remains binding unless narrowed.
Families touching trust, evidence, lifecycle reinterpretation, or authority movement remain review-sensitive.

family_gate_entries.title: Rollback-bearing staged transition validation
family_gate_entries.rationale: The family is the narrowest and most reversible bounded family, and its remaining blockers constrain scope rather than defeating bounded staged validation entirely.
required_guardrails: bounded to staged validation, rollback, downgrade, and refusal-aware transition rehearsal
no claim that runtime cutover proof is already closed
no live authority transfer
no live trust, revocation, or publication execution
unresolved receipt-pipeline limitations must remain visible in outputs

family_gate_entries.title: Bounded runtime cutover proof and rehearsal
family_gate_entries.rationale: The family is admissible only as proof-bearing rehearsal because cutover legality and stronger receipt continuity remain active blockers.
required_guardrails: rehearsal-only or proof-only posture unless a later checkpoint says otherwise
no authority handoff or state transfer
explicit proof packet obligations before any claim of lawful cutover
no inference of gate passage from protocol or runtime substrate alone
lifecycle-sensitive reinterpretation remains FULL review

family_gate_entries.title: Distributed replay and proof-anchor verification
family_gate_entries.rationale: The family reduces a central blocker through verification instead of live transfer, but continuity and replication proof still remain open.
required_guardrails: verification-only and no live authority movement
no claim that cross-shard logs or replication policies alone prove continuity
explicit proof-anchor continuity criteria remain missing until later work closes them
no claim that deterministic replication is already realized

family_gate_entries.title: Trust-aware refusal and containment
family_gate_entries.rationale: Bounded containment is safer than live trust execution, but the family still touches trust posture, refusal semantics, and remediation evidence.
required_guardrails: FULL review for trust and governance implications
refusal, containment, and remediation only
no trust-root rotation, revocation propagation, or publication execution
stronger receipt lineage required for any bounded trial

family_gate_entries.title: Live cutover receipt pipeline and anchorization
family_gate_entries.rationale: The family changes canonical evidence posture and cannot outrun the still-open receipt-pipeline and cutover-proof blockers.
required_guardrails: FULL review when canonical evidence semantics are reclassified
shadow or parallel evidence posture only
no claim that receipt-pipeline realization is complete
no promotion of local logs into canonical receipts

family_gate_entries.title: Bounded authority handoff and state transfer
family_gate_entries.rationale: The family is now doctrinally meaningful, but its proof and convergence dependencies are too central for first-wave scope.
required_guardrails: not first-wave
no authority transfer until deterministic replication, replay continuity, cutover proof, trust convergence, and stronger receipt posture materially narrow
no transport success treated as lawful handoff
no hidden local state transfer claims

family_gate_entries.title: Live trust, revocation, and publication execution
family_gate_entries.rationale: Live trust and publication execution remain unrealized, trust roots remain empty, and the blocker stack is too central to honest bounded entry.
required_guardrails: not admitted
no live trust execution
no live revocation propagation execution
no live publication execution
no claim that doctrinal operationalization gates equal realized execution systems

family_gate_entries.title: Live shard relocation
family_gate_entries.rationale: Readiness artifacts still mark shard relocation as unrealistic currently and the proof, trust, cutover, and receipt prerequisites remain far from satisfied.
required_guardrails: prohibited for bounded near-term entry
no shrink-wrapping relocation into first-wave wording
no claim that shard substrate proves relocation readiness

global_first_wave_guardrails: bounded entry must remain proof-oriented verification-oriented rehearsal-oriented or rollback-bearing
unresolved blockers remain attached and must not be restated as solved
no live authority handoff or state transfer
no live trust revocation or publication execution
code substrate registries or logs do not by themselves establish gate passage

doctrine_admission_realization_markers.meaning: Readiness for the Ζ-P admission band does not admit every family.
A family may have doctrine without being eligible for first-wave entry.
A narrowed blocker may still constrain or block gate passage.
Passing a gate does not mean the repo already has realized live machinery.

prohibited_gate_setting_shapes: admitting all families because zeta_p exists
ignoring unresolved blockers because doctrine exists
treating shard net trust or release code surfaces as gate passage
silently downgrading blocked families into later-wave scope without justification
silently downgrading prohibited perimeter families into bounded entry
downstream_refinement_prompts: bounded Ζ-A planning and admission prompts
conditional later Υ-D3 only if a later checkpoint finds genuine controller residue
